title: hieracium fulcratum subsp. ruebelianum
intro: Hieracium fulcratum subsp. ruebelianum is a rare and lesser-known plant species of the dandelion family, known scientifically as Asteraceae, and commonly referred to as a type of hawkweed. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits associated with Hieracium fulcratum subsp. ruebelianum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies. Herbal preparations of Hieracium fulcratum subsp. ruebelianum can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or by using the plant's extracts in the form of tinctures or ointments.
